As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is blinded for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Mine, Cryo (Average).name: Mine, Cryo (Average)
Mine, Cryo (Average).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 4d6]] cold damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature gains 1 slowed level for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Mine, cryo

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d6]] cold damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature gains 1 slowed level for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Jetpack (Improved).name: Jetpack (Improved)
Jetpack (Improved).description:
Adventuring Gear (Back)

Jetpacks are personal aerial transportation devices that allow the operator to fly into and through the air with great mobility. Activating or deactivating the jetpack requires a bonus action and, while active, you have a flying speed of 50 feet. The jetpack last for 1 minute per power cell (to a maximum of 14 minutes) and can be recharged by a power source or replacing the power cells.

 

Jetpack

Jetpacks are personal aerial transportation devices that allow the operator to fly into and through the air with great mobility. Activating or deactivating the jetpack requires a bonus action and, while active, you have a flying speed of 30 feet. The jetpack last for 1 minute per power cell (to a maximum of 10 minutes) and can be recharged by a power source or replacing the power cells.

Mine, Panic (Major).name: Mine, Panic (Major)
Mine, Panic (Major).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 6d6]] psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Mine, panic

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. Additionally, on a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Tool (Improved).name: Tool (Improved)
Tool (Improved).description:
Adventuring Gear

Your level of proficiency in these tools increases by three steps (to expertise, from trained to mastery, from proficient to high mastery, or from expertise to grand mastery).

Power Cell (Lethal).name: Power Cell (Lethal)
Power Cell (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls and deal an additional [[/r 1d8]] damage with this enhanced power cell. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the power cell is depleted, it no longer gives a bonus.

 

Power cell

Weapons with this property are fueled by power cells, which must be loaded in order to fire the weapon. A power cell fuels a number of attacks equal to the weapon's reload number.

Andris (High Quality).name: Andris (High Quality)
Andris (High Quality).description:
Consumable (Substance)

Refined andris crystal, a spice mined most commonly on Sevarcos II, sharpens a creature's mind when smoked. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next 10 minutes, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d8]] when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Wisdom. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 4 hours, during which they must roll a [[/r d8]] and subtract the result when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Wisdom. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Andris

Refined andris crystal, a spice mined most commonly on Sevarcos II, sharpens a creature's mind when smoked. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d3]] when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Wisdom. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, during which they must roll a [[/r d3]] and subtract the result when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Wisdom. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

Datacron Mk I.name: Datacron Mk I
Datacron Mk I.description:
Adventuring Gear

This datacron contains a single at-will tech power. After tinkering with the datacron for at least 2 hours, a techcaster with a Max Power Level of 1st or higher can make a DC 10 techcasting ability check with proficiency. On a success, they learn the contained tech power, which does not count against their tech powers known. On a failure, the power is not learned and the techcaster can't try again for 24 hours.

Datacrons are encrypted storage devices that contain an interactive projection, and are used to store high-value, sensitive information.

 

Datacron

A datacron is a type of holocron that can be accessed by non-Force-sensitives and are mainly used to store encrypted data. They are complete with an interactive projection to access the information.

Credit Chip (Champion).name: Credit Chip (Champion)
Credit Chip (Champion).description:
Adventuring Gear

The credit chip is a small, flat card that features a security code and algorithm memory stripes. The chip can be preloaded with a specified number of credits, or it can draw directly from a specific account held by the user. The credit chip can be accessed with a pin, or by a DC 30 slicer's kit check.

 

Credit chip

The chip can be preloaded with a specified number of credits, or it can draw directly from a specific account held by the user. The credit chip can be accessed with a pin, or by a DC 15 slicer's kit check.

Weapon (Any)

You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls and deal an additional [[/r 1d8]] damage with this enhanced weapon.

Machinist\u0027s Catalyzer Mk III.name: Machinist's Catalyzer Mk III
Machinist\u0027s Catalyzer Mk III.description:
Consumable (Technology)

As an action, you can administer this catalyzer to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this adrenal, the target gains a +2 bonus to its tech attack rolls, tech save DC, and damage and healing rolls with tech powers. This effect lasts for 1 minute. A creature can benefit from only one catalyzer at a time.

Intelligence Stim (Superior).name: Intelligence Stim (Superior)
Intelligence Stim (Superior).description:
Consumable (Stimpac)

As an action, you can administer this stimpac to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this stimpac, the target's Intelligence score increases by 6, and its maximum for this score increases by 6. This effect lasts for 1 hour. A creature can benefit from only one stimpac at a time.

Charisma Stim (Basic).name: Charisma Stim (Basic)
Charisma Stim (Basic).description:
Consumable (Stimpac)

As an action, you can administer this stimpac to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this stimpac, the target's Charisma score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20. This effect lasts for 1 hour. A creature can benefit from only one stimpac at a time.

Flux Collimator (Deadly).name: Flux Collimator (Deadly)
Flux Collimator (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Flux collimator

Repair Kit Mk III.name: Repair Kit Mk III
Repair Kit Mk III.description:
Consumable (Technology)

A repair kit included the basic tools needed to repair a droid after being damaged in combat. The kit has three uses. As an action, you can expend one use of the kit to restore hit points to a droid or construct within 5 feet. The creature rolls three dice equal to the size of their Hit Die and regains hit points equal to the amount rolled + their Constitution modifier (minimum of one hit point). If the creature has Hit Dice of different sizes, use whichever Hit Die size they have the most of.

If this would reduce your maximum hit points below 1, it instead becomes 1. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Tihaar

This colorless spirit, favored by Mandalorians, burns the mouth and throat when consumed. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d3]] when making an ability check or saving throw using Constitution, and at the start of each of the creature's turns it gains [[/r 2d3]] temporary hit points. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, during which they must roll a [[/r d3]] and subtract the result when making an ability check or saving throw using Constitution, and for the duration their current and maximum hit points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. If this would reduce your maximum hit points below 1, it instead becomes 1. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw to resist addiction.

Flechette Clip, Fragmentation (Lethal).name: Flechette Clip, Fragmentation (Lethal)
Flechette Clip, Fragmentation (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced wrist launcher ammunition fires a volley of kinetic flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette clip holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 5d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. If you lack proficiency in the wrist launcher, you must roll the damage dice twice and take the lesser total.

 

Flechette clip, fragmentation

When triggered, this wrist launcher ammunition fires a volley of kinetic flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette clip holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 1d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. If you lack proficiency in the wrist launcher, you have disadvantage on damage rolls with it

Wristpad Chassis (Standard).name: Wristpad Chassis (Standard)
Wristpad Chassis (Standard).description:
Focus (Tech)

Requires attunement

This modifiable wristpad chassis comes with vacant modification slots for wristpad modifications. It can house modifications of standard rarity.

 

Wristpad

A wristpad is a harness with an integrated datapad and holoprojector interface that fits on the forearm and includes self-charging battery packs.

Missile, Fragmentation (Average).name: Missile, Fragmentation (Average)
Missile, Fragmentation (Average).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this enhanced wrist launcher ammunition, which deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 2d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

 

Missile, fragmentation

This wrist launcher ammunition deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 1d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

Missile, Fragmentation (Major).name: Missile, Fragmentation (Major)
Missile, Fragmentation (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +2 bonus to attack rolls made with this enhanced wrist launcher ammunition, which deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 3d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

 

Missile, fragmentation

This wrist launcher ammunition deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 1d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

Mine, Stun (Average).name: Mine, Stun (Average)
Mine, Stun (Average).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is stunned until the end of their next turn.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until someone uses an action to shake or slap the creature awake. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Mine, stun

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is stunned until the end of their next turn.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until someone uses an action to shake or slap the creature awake. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Environmental Barrier Mk V.name: Environmental Barrier Mk V
Environmental Barrier Mk V.description:
Consumable (Barrier)

As an action, you can administer this barrier to a creature within 5 feet. The barrier has 75 hit points, and lasts 8 hours. Whenever the creature takes damage (one of acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, or sonic, chosen by the GM when the item is created), the barrier takes the damage instead. If this damage reduces the barrier to 0 hit points, you take any remaining damage. A creature can benefit from only one barrier at a time.

Sith Lord\u0027s Robe.name: Sith Lord's Robe
Sith Lord\u0027s Robe.description:
Adventuring Gear (Body)

Requires attunement

You gain a +3 bonus to damage rolls with weapons and force powers while you wear this robe.

Designed for those who relish personal combat, and know that power comes to those who take it, these robes masterfully augment offensive capabilities with no hindrance to movement.

Electrifying Collimator (Deadly).name: Electrifying Collimator (Deadly)
Electrifying Collimator (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the shocking 16 property. If it already has the shocking property, its shocking number increases by 4, to a maximum of 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Electrifying collimator

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the shocking 13 property. If it already has the shocking property, its shocking number increases by 1, to a maximum of 18.

Focus Generator Chassis (Prototype).name: Focus Generator Chassis (Prototype)
Focus Generator Chassis (Prototype).description:
Focus (Force)

Requires attunement

This modifiable focus generator chassis comes with vacant modification slots for focus generator modifications, as well as one augment slot. It can house modifications of prototype rarity or lower.

 

Focus generator

A focus generator is a handheld object used to help forcecasters learn to focus on their powers.

Handwraps Chassis (Legendary).name: Handwraps Chassis (Legendary)
Handwraps Chassis (Legendary).description:
Weapon (Unarmed Strike)

Requires attunement

These modifiable handwraps chassis comes with vacant modification slots for vibroweapon modifications, as well as two augment slots. They can house modifications of legendary rarity or lower.

Baragwin Stealth Unit.name: Baragwin Stealth Unit
Baragwin Stealth Unit.description:
Adventuring Gear (Waist)

Requires attunement

While wearing this belt, you can turn on its stealth field to cause yourself to become invisible. While you are invisible, anything you are carrying or wearing is invisible with you. You become visible when you turn off the field. Turning the field on or off requires an action.

Deduct the time you are invisible, in increments of 1 minute, from the belt's maximum duration of 2 hours. After 2 hours of use, the belt ceases to function. For every uninterrupted period of 12 hours the belt goes unused, it regains 1 hour of use.

Those Baragwin who sought to turn their telepathic empathy into an asset for bounty hunting needed something special to overcome the drawbacks of their lumbering frames.

Medpac (Exceptional).name: Medpac (Exceptional)
Medpac (Exceptional).description:
Consumable (Medpac)

A medpac is a quick-acting syringe filled with a concentrated dose of kolto. As an action, you can use this medpac to restore hit points to a beast or humanoid within 5 feet. The creature rolls five dice equal to the size of their Hit Die and regains hit points equal to the amount rolled + their Constitution modifier (minimum of one hit point). If the creature has Hit Dice of different sizes, use whichever Hit Die size they have the most of.

 

Medpac

A medpac is a quick-acting syringe filled with a concentrated dose of kolto. As an action, you can use this medpac to restore hit points to a beast or humanoid within 5 feet. The creature rolls one die equal to the size of their Hit Die and regains hit points equal to the amount rolled + their Constitution modifier (minimum of one hit point). If the creature has Hit Dice of different sizes, use whichever Hit Die size they have the most of.

Electrifying Calibrator (Average).name: Electrifying Calibrator (Average)
Electrifying Calibrator (Average).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the shocking 14 property. If it already has the shocking property, its shocking number increases by 2, to a maximum of 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Electrifying calibrator

This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the shocking 13 property. Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until someone uses an action to shake or slap the creature awake. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Cryo Cell (Devastating).name: Cryo Cell (Devastating)
Cryo Cell (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced cryo cell. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the cryo cell is depleted, it no longer gives a bonus. Additionally, the loaded weapon deals cold damage instead of its normal damage type, and when you hit with a shot the creature must make a DC 24 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d8]] cold damage and gains 1 slowed level until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't slowed. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, that creature is frozen in carbonite for 1 hour.

 

Cryo cell

A cryo cell is a specialized power cell for use with blaster weapons that deal acid, energy, fire, ion, or sonic damage. The loaded weapon deals cold damage instead of its normal damage type, and when you hit with a shot the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d4]] cold damage and gains 1 slowed level until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't slowed. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, that creature is frozen in carbonite for 1 hour.

Rocket, Ion (Lethal).name: Rocket, Ion (Lethal)
Rocket, Ion (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls made with this enhanced rocket launcher ammunition, which deals [[/r 3d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, hit or miss, the rocket then explodes. The target and each creature within 15 feet must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 10d6]] ion damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

 

Rocket, ion

This rocket launcher ammunition deals [[/r 2d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Additionally, hit or miss, the rocket then explodes. The target and each creature within 15 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 2d6]] ion damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

Power Generator (Lethal).name: Power Generator (Lethal)
Power Generator (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls and deal an additional [[/r 1d8]] damage with this enhanced power generator. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the torpedo is depleted, it no longer gives a bonus.

 

Power generator

Power generators energize the blaster cannon.

Deafening Cell (Devastating).name: Deafening Cell (Devastating)
Deafening Cell (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced deafening cell. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the deafening cell is depleted, it no longer gives a bonus. Additionally, the loaded weapon deals sonic damage instead of its normal damage type, and when you hit with a shot the creature must make a DC 24 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d10]] sonic damage and is deafened until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't deafened.

 

Deafening cell

A deafening cell is a specialized power cell for use with blaster weapons that deal acid, energy, fire, ion, or sonic damage. The loaded weapon deals sonic damage instead of its normal damage type, and when you hit with a shot the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d6]] sonic damage and is deafened until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't deafened.

Repair Kit Mk IV.name: Repair Kit Mk IV
Repair Kit Mk IV.description:
Consumable (Technology)

A repair kit included the basic tools needed to repair a droid after being damaged in combat. The kit has three uses. As an action, you can expend one use of the kit to restore hit points to a droid or construct within 5 feet. The creature rolls four dice equal to the size of their Hit Die and regains hit points equal to the amount rolled + their Constitution modifier (minimum of one hit point). If the creature has Hit Dice of different sizes, use whichever Hit Die size they have the most of.

 

Repair kit

A repair kit included the basic tools needed to repair a droid or construct after being damaged in combat. The kit has three uses. As an action, you can expend one use of the kit to restore hit points to a droid or construct within 5 feet. The creature rolls one die equal to the size of their Hit Die and regains hit points equal

Blueprint (Artifact).name: Blueprint (Artifact)
Blueprint (Artifact).description:
Adventuring Gear

This blueprint contains information regarding the raw and rare materials, tools, and steps required to craft an item of artifact rarity. Over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a short rest, a character can attempt to learn the blueprint. If they do so, they must make a DC 30 Intelligence check with the appropriate tools. On a success, the blueprint for the item is learned. On a failure, the blueprint is not learned, and the character must wait 24 hours before they can try again.

Blueprints contain the secrets of how to craft items of a more complex nature.

Rocket Boots (Exceptional).name: Rocket Boots (Exceptional)
Rocket Boots (Exceptional).description:
Adventuring Gear (Feet)

Rocket boots are a form of rocket propulsion system affixed to a pair of boots instead of being worn on the back like a standard jetpack. Activating or deactivating the boots requires a bonus action and, while active, you have a flying speed of 45 feet. The rocket boots last for 1 minute and can be recharged by a power source or replacing the power cell.

 

Rocket boots

Rocket boots are a form of rocket propulsion system affixed to a pair of boots instead of being worn on the back like a standard jetpack. Activating or deactivating the boots requires a bonus action and, while active, you have a flying speed of 25 feet. The rocket boots last for 1 minute and can be recharged by a power source or replacing the power cells.

Lightweapon Chassis (Prototype).name: Lightweapon Chassis (Prototype)
Lightweapon Chassis (Prototype).description:
Weapon (Any Lightweapon)

Requires attunement

This modifiable lightweapon chassis comes with vacant modification slots for lightweapon modifications, as well as one augment slot. It can house modifications of prototype rarity or lower.

Karrak (Pure).name: Karrak (Pure)
Karrak (Pure).description:
Consumable (Substance)

A powerful painkiller, karrak allows creatures to continue through the most grievous injuries. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next hour, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d12]] when making an ability check or saving throw using Constitution, and at the start of each of the creature's turns it gains [[/r 2d12]] temporary hit points. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 24 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 8 hours, during which they must roll a [[/r d12]] and subtract the result when making an ability check or saving throw using Constitution, and for the duration their current and maximum hit points are reduced by [[/r 2d12]]. If this would reduce your maximum hit points below 1, it instead becomes 1. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 24 Constitution throw to resist addiction.

 

Karrak

A powerful painkiller, karrak allows creatures to continue through the most grievous injuries. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d3]] when making an ability check or saving throw using Constitution, and at the start of each of the creature's turns it gains [[/r 2d3]] temporary hit points. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, during which they must roll a [[/r d3]] and subtract the result when making an ability check or saving throw using Constitution, and for the duration their current and maximum hit points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. If this would reduce your maximum hit points below 1, it instead becomes 1. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

Grenade, Plasma (Major).name: Grenade, Plasma (Major)
Grenade, Plasma (Major).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 6d10]] energy damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

 

Grenade, plasma

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d10]] energy damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Shoulder Cannon (Champion).name: Shoulder Cannon (Champion)
Shoulder Cannon (Champion).description:
Weapon (Shoulder Cannon)

Mounted in the shoulder slot, a shoulder cannon does not require a free hand to use. Additionally, you have advantage on Strength ability checks and saving throws to avoid being disarmed of this weapon. You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls and deal an additional [[/r 1d10]] damage with this enhanced shoulder cannon, which has the smart (20, +6) property.

 

Shoulder cannon

Auto

Weapons with this property can only fire in burst, penetrating, or rapid modes. If the weapon has any combination of burst, penetrating, and rapid modes, you choose which mode to use. You can only use one mode at a time.

Burst 8

When you would make a ranged weapon attack with a weapon with this property, you can instead spray a 10-foot-cube area within range with shots, consuming ammunition equal to the burst number. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your bonus to attacks with the weapon) or take the weapon's normal damage. Before you use this property, you can declare one creature that would be affected by it. If the creature fails its save, in addition to the weapon's normal damage, it is subjected to any additional effects that would occur on a hit. If you would have advantage on your attack roll, the creature instead has disadvantage on their saving throw, and if you would have disadvantage on your attack roll, the creature instead has advantage on their saving throw.

Power cell (range 60/240) Cell

Weapons with this property are fueled by power cells, which must be loaded in order to fire the weapon. A power cell fuels a number of attacks equal to the weapon's reload number.

Reload 8

A number of shots equal to the reload number can be made with a weapon with this property. A character must then reload it using an action or a bonus action (their choice). You must have one free hand to reload.

Smart (15/+2)

This weapon ignores your Dexterity score and proficiency bonus. Two numbers appear in parentheses with the property—the first is the weapon's Dexterity score, and the second is the weapon's proficiency bonus. The weapon uses these values instead of yours when you make attack and damage rolls with it. If you lack proficiency with the weapon, the weapon does not benefit from its proficiency bonus.

Strength 11

A weapon with this property has a heavy kickback. While wielding it, you have disadvantage on attack rolls unless you meet the Strength requirement. If the weapon has the burst, penetrating, or rapid property, you must meet the Strength requirement to use this feature. If the weapon lacks the two-handed property, you can wield the weapon in two hands to temporarily reduce the strength number by one step (from 19 to 17, 17 to 15, 15 to 13, or 13 to 11). If this would reduce the strength number below 11, you ignore it entirely.

Special

Mounted by the shoulder slot, a shoulder cannon does not require a free hand to use. Additionally, you have advantage on Strength ability checks and saving throws to avoid being disarmed of this weapon.

Gas Cartridge (Lethal).name: Gas Cartridge (Lethal)
Gas Cartridge (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced gas cartridge. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the gas cartridge has been fired, it no longer gives a bonus. Additionally, when you hit with a shot with the loaded weapon, the creature must make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d8]] poison damage and is poisoned until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't poisoned.

 

Gas cartridge

A gas cartridge is a specialized slug cartridge for use with blaster weapons that deal kinetic damage. When you hit with a shot with the loaded weapon, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d6]] poison damage and is poisoned until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't poisoned.

Rocket, Incendiary (Deadly).name: Rocket, Incendiary (Deadly)
Rocket, Incendiary (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls made with this enhanced rocket launcher ammunition, which deals [[/r 2d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, hit or miss, the rocket then explodes, setting the ground beneath it in a 15 foot radius ablaze for 1 minute. When a creature enters the fire or starts its turn there it must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 8d8]] fire damage or half as much on a successful one. A construct makes this save with disadvantage.

 

Rocket, incendiary

This rocket launcher ammunition deals [[/r 2d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Additionally, hit or miss, the rocket then explodes, setting the ground beneath it in a 15 foot radius ablaze for 1 minute. When a creature enters the fire or starts its turn there it must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 2d8]] fire damage or half as much on a successful one. A construct makes this save with disadvantage.

Clothes, Clandestine (Champion).name: Clothes, Clandestine (Champion)
Clothes, Clandestine (Champion).description:
Adventuring Gear (Body)

These clothes come with nine hidden pockets, each of which can store up to 1 lb. Finding these hidden pockets requires a DC 30 Investigation check.

 

Clothes, clandestine

These clothes come with two hidden pockets, each of which can store up to 1.00 lb. Finding these hidden pockets requires a DC 15 Investigation check.

Oscillation Calibrator (Average).name: Oscillation Calibrator (Average)
Oscillation Calibrator (Average).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Oscillation calibrator

Remote Detonator (Champion).name: Remote Detonator (Champion)
Remote Detonator (Champion).description:
Adventuring Gear

A small handhold device with a single button, used to activate explosives. Over the course of 1 minute, you can pair the detonator with up to 12 mines within 10 feet. As an action, you can remotely detonate all of the paired mines. You must be within 1,000 feet of the mines to detonate them.

 

Remote detonator

A small handhold device with a single button, used to activate explosives. Over the course of 1 minute, you can pair the detonator with up to two mines within 10 feet. As an action, you can remotely detonate all of the paired mines. You must be within 30 feet of the mines to detonate them.

Weapon (Superior).name: Weapon (Superior)
Weapon (Superior).description:
Weapon (Any)

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced weapon.

As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 6d6]] sonic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is deafened for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Grenade, deafening

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d6]] sonic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is deafened for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Wristpad Chassis (Premium).name: Wristpad Chassis (Premium)
Wristpad Chassis (Premium).description:
Focus (Tech)

Requires attunement

This modifiable wristpad chassis comes with vacant modification slots for wristpad modifications. It can house modifications of premium rarity or lower.

 

Wristpad

A wristpad is a harness with an integrated datapad and holoprojector interface that fits on the forearm and includes self-charging battery packs.

Alacrity Adrenal (Exceptional).name: Alacrity Adrenal (Exceptional)
Alacrity Adrenal (Exceptional).description:
Consumable (Adrenal)

As an action, you can administer this adrenal to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this adrenal, the target's speed increases by 50 feet. This effect lasts for 1 minute. A creature can benefit from only one adrenal at a time.

Cryo Cell (Major).name: Cryo Cell (Major)
Cryo Cell (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced cryo cell. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the cryo cell is depleted, it no longer gives a bonus. Additionally, the loaded weapon deals cold damage instead of its normal damage type, and when you hit with a shot the creature must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d4]] cold damage and gains 1 slowed level until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't slowed. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, that creature is frozen in carbonite for 1 hour.

 

Cryo cell

A cryo cell is a specialized power cell for use with blaster weapons that deal acid, energy, fire, ion, or sonic damage. The loaded weapon deals cold damage instead of its normal damage type, and when you hit with a shot the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d4]] cold damage and gains 1 slowed level until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't slowed. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, that creature is frozen in carbonite for 1 hour.

Deafening Calibrator (Lethal).name: Deafening Calibrator (Lethal)
Deafening Calibrator (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dealing an additional [[/r 1d8]] damage for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the sonorous 17 property. If it already has the sonorous property, its sonorous number increases by 5, to a maximum of 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Deafening calibrator

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the sonorous 13 property. If it already has the sonorous property, its sonorous number increases by 1, to a maximum of 18.

Missile, Ion (Major).name: Missile, Ion (Major)
Missile, Ion (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +2 bonus to attack rolls made with this enhanced wrist launcher ammunition, which deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 3d6]] ion damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

 

Missile, ion

This wrist launcher ammunition deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 1d6]] ion damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

Ring of Tech Storing Mk I.name: Ring of Tech Storing Mk I
Ring of Tech Storing Mk I.description:
Adventuring Gear (Finger)

Requires attunement

This ring stores tech powers cast into it, holding them until the attuned wearer uses them. The ring can store up to 2 levels worth of tech powers at a time. When found, it contains [[/r 1d3]] - 1 levels of stored tech powers chosen by the GM.

Any creature can cast a tech power of 1st or 2nd level into the ring by touching the ring as the tech power is cast. The tech power has no effect, other than to be stored in the ring. If the ring can't hold the tech power, the tech power is expended without effect. The level of the slot used to cast the tech power determines how much space it uses.

While wearing this ring, you can cast any tech power stored in it. The tech power uses the slot level, tech save DC, tech attack bonus, and techcasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the tech power. The tech power cast from the ring is no longer stored in it, freeing up space.

Panic Collimator (Devastating).name: Panic Collimator (Devastating)
Panic Collimator (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dealing an additional [[/r 1d10]] damage for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the neuralizing 18 property. If it already has the neuralizing property, its neuralizing number increases to 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Panic collimator

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the neuralizing 13 property. If it already has the neuralizing property, its neuralizing number increases by 1, to a maximum of 18.

Electrifying Calibrator (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the shocking 15 property. If it already has the shocking property, its shocking number increases by 3, to a maximum of 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Electrifying calibrator

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the shocking 13 property. If it already has the shocking property, its shocking number increases by 1, to a maximum of 18.

Hewey.name: Hewey
Hewey.description:
Weapon (Vibroblade)

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this enhanced weapon.

This weapon has 6 charges and regains [[/r 1d6]] charges when you complete a long rest. Once per turn when you hit with a melee weapon attack with this weapon, you can expend a charge to have the weapon deal an additional [[/r 1d8]] kinetic damage.

Hewey is an N-30 battle blade owned by the mercenary Corso Riggs during the Galactic Cold War.

 

Vibroblade

Versatile ([[/r 1d10]])

A weapon with this property can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Smugglepack (Fine).name: Smugglepack (Fine)
Smugglepack (Fine).description:
Adventuring Gear (Back)

This backpack comes with a main compartment that stores 14 lb., not exceeding 1/2 cubic foot. Additionally, it has a hidden storage compartment that stores 6 lb., not exceeding 1/4 cubic foot. Finding the hidden compartment requires a DC 18 Investigation check.

 

Smugglepack

This backpack comes with a main compartment that stores 15.00 lb., not exceeding 1/2 cubic foot. Additionally, it has a hidden storage compartment that stores 5 lb., not exceeding 1/4 cubic foot. Finding the hidden compartment requires a DC 15 Investigation check.

Armor Chassis (Advanced).name: Armor Chassis (Advanced)
Armor Chassis (Advanced).description:
Armor (Any)

Requires attunement

This modifiable armor chassis comes with vacant modification slots for armor modifications, as well as one augment slot. It can house modifications of advanced rarity or lower.

Missile, Fragmentation (Devastating).name: Missile, Fragmentation (Devastating)
Missile, Fragmentation (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls made with this enhanced wrist launcher ammunition, which deals [[/r 3d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 24 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 6d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

 

Missile, fragmentation

This wrist launcher ammunition deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 1d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

Muon Gold (Fine).name: Muon Gold (Fine)
Muon Gold (Fine).description:
Consumable (Substance)

Muon gold is a lubricant-based spice that gives users intensified mental clarity and focus for a short time. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d4]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 1 hour, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d4]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Muon gold

Muon gold is a lubricant-based spice that gives users intensified mental clarity and focus for a short time. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

Handwraps Chassis (Standard).description:
Weapon (Unarmed Strike)

Requires attunement

These modifiable handwraps chassis comes with vacant modification slots for vibroweapon modifications. They can house modifications of standard rarity.

Alacrity Adrenal (Fine).name: Alacrity Adrenal (Fine)
Alacrity Adrenal (Fine).description:
Consumable (Adrenal)

As an action, you can administer this adrenal to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this adrenal, the target's speed increases by 20 feet. This effect lasts for 1 minute. A creature can benefit from only one adrenal at a time.

Panic Dart (Devastating).name: Panic Dart (Devastating)
Panic Dart (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this wrist launcher dart, which deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the dart has been fired, it no longer gives a bonus. Also on a hit, the creature must make a DC 24 Wisdom saving throw, taking [[/r 1d8]] psychic damage on a failed save.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Panic dart

This wrist launcher ammunition deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Also on a hit, the creature must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw, taking [[/r 1d4]] psychic damage on a failed save.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Shoulder Cannon (Fine).name: Shoulder Cannon (Fine)
Shoulder Cannon (Fine).description:
Weapon (Shoulder Cannon)

Mounted in the shoulder slot, a shoulder cannon does not require a free hand to use. Additionally, you have advantage on Strength ability checks and saving throws to avoid being disarmed of this weapon. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced shoulder cannon, which has the smart (16, +2) property.

 

Shoulder cannon

Auto

Weapons with this property can only fire in burst, penetrating, or rapid modes. If the weapon has any combination of burst, penetrating, and rapid modes, you choose which mode to use. You can only use one mode at a time.

Burst 8

When you would make a ranged weapon attack with a weapon with this property, you can instead spray a 10-foot-cube area within range with shots, consuming ammunition equal to the burst number. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your bonus to attacks with the weapon) or take the weapon's normal damage. Before you use this property, you can declare one creature that would be affected by it. If the creature fails its save, in addition to the weapon's normal damage, it is subjected to any additional effects that would occur on a hit. If you would have advantage on your attack roll, the creature instead has disadvantage on their saving throw, and if you would have disadvantage on your attack roll, the creature instead has advantage on their saving throw.

Power cell (range 60/240) Cell

Weapons with this property are fueled by power cells, which must be loaded in order to fire the weapon. A power cell fuels a number of attacks equal to the weapon's reload number.

Reload 8

A number of shots equal to the reload number can be made with a weapon with this property. A character must then reload it using an action or a bonus action (their choice). You must have one free hand to reload.

Smart (15/+2)

This weapon ignores your Dexterity score and proficiency bonus. Two numbers appear in parentheses with the property—the first is the weapon's Dexterity score, and the second is the weapon's proficiency bonus. The weapon uses these values instead of yours when you make attack and damage rolls with it. If you lack proficiency with the weapon, the weapon does not benefit from its proficiency bonus.

Strength 11

A weapon with this property has a heavy kickback. While wielding it, you have disadvantage on attack rolls unless you meet the Strength requirement. If the weapon has the burst, penetrating, or rapid property, you must meet the Strength requirement to use this feature. If the weapon lacks the two-handed property, you can wield the weapon in two hands to temporarily reduce the strength number by one step (from 19 to 17, 17 to 15, 15 to 13, or 13 to 11). If this would reduce the strength number below 11, you ignore it entirely.

Special

Mounted by the shoulder slot, a shoulder cannon does not require a free hand to use. Additionally, you have advantage on Strength ability checks and saving throws to avoid being disarmed of this weapon.

Holocron (Ancient).name: Holocron (Ancient)
Holocron (Ancient).description:
Adventuring Gear

This holocron contains a single 9th-level force power, or multiple powers of 1st-8th level with a combined level no greater than 9. After meditating with the holocron for at least 80 hours, a forcecaster with a Max Power Level of 9th or higher can make a DC 30 forcecasting ability check with proficiency based on the alignment of the power(s) stored within. On a success, they learn the contained force power(s), which does not count against their force powers known and can not be unlearned except through use of a remove curse power. On a failure, the power is not learned and the forcecaster can't try again for 24 hours.

Holocrons are information-storage devices used by Force-wielders that contain ancient lessons or valuable information in holographic form.

 

Holocron

Holocrons are information-storage devices used by force wielders that contain ancient lessons or valuable information in holographic form. They appear as palm-sized, glowing polyhedrons of crystalline material and hardware, and can only be activated and used through the power of the Force.

Binders (Superior).name: Binders (Superior)
Binders (Superior).description:
Adventuring Gear

Binders can be used to restrain a Small or Medium creature with appropriate appendages. Applying binders takes an action, and the target must make a DC 20 Strength or Dexterity saving throw (the target chooses the ability to use). A hostile creature makes this save with advantage. A set of binders comes with one key. Breaking the binders requires a DC 26 Strength check. Escaping them requires a DC 26 Dexterity check. Without the key, unlocking the binders requires a DC 24 security kit to force open. Binders have an AC of 13 and 60 hit points. Destroying the binders frees the creature without harming it.

 

Binders

Binders can be used to restrain a Small or Medium creature with appropriate appendages. Applying binders takes an action, and the target must make a DC 14 Strength or Dexterity saving throw (the target chooses the ability to use). A hostile creature makes this save with advantage. Breaking the binders requires a DC 20 Strength check. Escaping them requires a DC 20 Dexterity check. A set of binders comes with one key. Without the key, unlocking the binders requires a DC 15 security kit to force open. Binders have an AC of 10 and 15 hit points. Destroying the binders frees the creature without harming it.

Deafening Cell (Average).name: Deafening Cell (Average)
Deafening Cell (Average).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced deafening cell. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the deafening cell is depleted, it no longer gives a bonus. Additionally, the loaded weapon deals sonic damage instead of its normal damage type, and when you hit with a shot the creature must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d6]] sonic damage and is deafened until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't deafened.

 

Deafening cell

A deafening cell is a specialized power cell for use with blaster weapons that deal acid, energy, fire, ion, or sonic damage. The loaded weapon deals sonic damage instead of its normal damage type, and when you hit with a shot the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d6]] sonic damage and is deafened until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't deafened.

Yaladai (Potent).name: Yaladai (Potent)
Yaladai (Potent).description:
Consumable (Substance)

Yaladai is a powerful stimulant that grants extreme clarity, improving relaxation and focus. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next hour, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum force points by [[/r 2d10]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 4 hours, their current and maximum force points are reduced by [[/r 2d10]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Yaladai

Yaladai is a powerful stimulant that grants extreme clarity, improving relaxation and focus. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum force points by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, their current and maximum force points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

AB-75 Bo-Rifle.name: AB-75 Bo-Rifle
AB-75 Bo-Rifle.description:
Weapon (Bo-Rifle)

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this enhanced weapon. Additionally, while it is in staff mode, the save DC for its shocking property increases to 15.

This version of the bo-rifle was exclusive to high-ranking members of the Honor Guard of Lasan.

 

Bo-rifle

Modal (blaster rifle and techstaff)

A weapon with this property can switch between modes. A list of weapons in parentheses appears with the property-the weapons between which an exotic weapon can be switched by using an object interaction.

Additionally, hit or miss, the rocket then explodes, setting the ground beneath it in a 15 foot radius ablaze for 1 minute. When a creature enters the fire or starts its turn there it must make a DC 24 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 12d8]] fire damage or half as much on a successful one. A construct makes this save with disadvantage.

 

Rocket, incendiary

Blaster Chassis (Prototype).description:
Weapon (Any Blaster)

Requires attunement

This modifiable blaster chassis comes with vacant modification slots for blaster modifications, as well as one augment slot. It can house modifications of prototype rarity or lower.

Consumable (Ammunition)

Security Spike (Superior).name: Security Spike (Superior)
Security Spike (Superior).description:
Consumable (Technology)

When you make an Intelligence (security kit) check, you can use the spike (no action required) to gain advantage on the check, and then you can reroll one of the dice once. You can wait until after you roll the [[/r d20]] before deciding to use the spike, but you must decide before the GM says whether the check succeeds or fails.

 

Security spike

When you make an Intelligence (security kit) check, you can use the spike (no action required) to reroll the check. You must use the new roll. You can wait until after you roll the [[/r d20]] before deciding to use the spike, but you must decide before the GM says whether the check succeeds or fails.

Mine, Flash (Lethal).name: Mine, Flash (Lethal)
Mine, Flash (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is blinded for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Mine, flash

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Glitterstim (Fine).name: Glitterstim (Fine)
Glitterstim (Fine).description:
Consumable (Substance)

A silvery-green webbing sharp to the touch, yet when liquidized causes a heightened mental state and pleasurable boost. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d4]] when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Intelligence. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 1 hour, during which they must roll a [[/r d4]] and subtract the result when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Intelligence. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Glitterstim

At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution

saving throw to resist addiction.
