It ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

 

Projector tank, incendiary

When triggered, this vapor projector ammunition produces a burst of flame in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel tank holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 2d8]] fire damage or half as much on a successful one. The fire spreads around corners. It ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

Projector Tank, Cryo (Deadly).name: Projector Tank, Cryo (Deadly)
Projector Tank, Cryo (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced vapor projector ammunition produces a beam of carbonite energy in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel tank holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes [[/r 5d4]] cold damage and gains 1 slowed level until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't slowed. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, that creature is frozen in carbonite for 1 hour.

 

Projector tank, cryo

When triggered, this vapor projector ammunition produces a beam of carbonite energy in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel tank holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes [[/r 2d4]] cold damage and gains 1 slowed level until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't slowed. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, that creature is frozen in carbonite for 1 hour.

Tool (Champion).name: Tool (Champion)
Tool (Champion).description:
Adventuring Gear

You have grand mastery in these tools.

Flechette Mag, Ion (Average).name: Flechette Mag, Ion (Average)
Flechette Mag, Ion (Average).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced flechette cannon ammunition fires a volley of ion flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette mag holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 3d6]] ion damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

 

Flechette mag, ion

When triggered, this flechette cannon ammunition fires a volley of ion flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette mag holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d6]] ion damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

Bolas (Exceptional).name: Bolas (Exceptional)
Bolas (Exceptional).description:
Weapon (Bolas)

A Large or smaller creature hit by a bolas is restrained until it is freed. A bolas has no effect on formless or Huge or larger creatures. A creature can use its action to make a DC 17 Dexterity check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. The bolas has an AC of 14, 25 hit points, immunity to all damage not dealt by melee weapons, and resistance to damage dealt by unenhanced melee weapons. Destroying the bolas frees the creature without harming it and immediately ends the bolas's effects. While a creature is restrained by a bolas, you can make no further attacks with it.

Due to their unwieldy nature, bolas make ineffective melee weapons. Melee attack rolls made with them are made at disadvantage.

 

Bolas

Light

A weapon with this property is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when engaging in Double- or Two-Weapon Fighting. See the rules for Double- and Two-Weapon Fighting in chapter 9.

Thrown (range 20/60)

A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has this property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's long range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.

Special

A Large or smaller creature hit by a bolas is restrained until it is freed. A bolas has no effect on formless or Huge or larger creatures. A creature can use its action to make a DC 13 Dexterity check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. The bolas has an AC of 10, 5 hit points, and immunity to all damage not dealt by melee weapons. Destroying the bolas frees the creature without harming it and immediately ends the bolas's effects. While a creature is restrained by a bolas, you can make no further attacks with it.

Due to their unwieldy nature, bolas make ineffective melee weapons. Melee attack rolls made with them are made at disadvantage.

Stamina Adrenal (Superior).name: Stamina Adrenal (Superior)
Stamina Adrenal (Superior).description:
Consumable (Adrenal)

As an action, you can administer this adrenal to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this adrenal, the target's current and maximum hit points increase by 40. This effect lasts for 1 minute. A creature can benefit from only one adrenal at a time.

Jedi Master\u0027s Robe.name: Jedi Master's Robe
Jedi Master\u0027s Robe.description:
Adventuring Gear (Body)

Requires attunement

You gain a +3 bonus to AC while you wear this robe.

Members of the Order typically wear plain or unassuming garments, but Jedi Masters also know the importance of adequate protection when great challenges must be surmounted.

Muon Gold (Uncut).name: Muon Gold (Uncut)
Muon Gold (Uncut).description:
Consumable (Substance)

Muon gold is a lubricant-based spice that gives users intensified mental clarity and focus for a short time. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next 10 minutes, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d6]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 1 hour, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d6]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Muon gold

Muon gold is a lubricant-based spice that gives users intensified mental clarity and focus for a short time. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

Deafening Collimator (Major).name: Deafening Collimator (Major)
Deafening Collimator (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the sonorous 15 property. If it already has the sonorous property, its sonorous number increases by 3, to a maximum of 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Deafening collimator

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the sonorous 13 property. If it already has the sonorous property, its sonorous number increases by 1, to a maximum of 18.

Armor (Fine).name: Armor (Fine)
Armor (Fine).description:
Armor (Any)

You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

Flashy.name: Flashy
Flashy.description:
Weapon (Heavy Pistol)

The first time you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack from this weapon each round, the creature takes an additional [[/r 1d4]] damage from the attack.

Flashy is an SSK heavy pistol owned by the mercenary Corso Riggs during the Galactic Cold War.

 

Heavy pistol

Power cell (range 45/180) Cell

Weapons with this property are fueled by power cells, which must be loaded in order to fire the weapon. A power cell fuels a number of attacks equal to the weapon's reload number.

Reload 16

A number of shots equal to the reload number can be made with a weapon with this property. A character must then reload it using an action or a bonus action (their choice). You must have one free hand to reload.

Environmental Barrier Mk I.name: Environmental Barrier Mk I
Environmental Barrier Mk I.description:
Consumable (Barrier)

As an action, you can administer this barrier to a creature within 5 feet. The barrier has 15 hit points, and lasts 8 hours. Whenever the creature takes damage (one of acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, or sonic, chosen by the GM when the item is created), the barrier takes the damage instead. If this damage reduces the barrier to 0 hit points, the creature takes any remaining damage. A creature can benefit from only one barrier at a time.

Armor Chassis (Premium).name: Armor Chassis (Premium)
Armor Chassis (Premium).description:
Armor (Any)

Requires attunement

This modifiable armor chassis comes with vacant modification slots for armor modifications. It can house modifications of premium rarity or lower.

Mine, Plasma (Lethal).name: Mine, Plasma (Lethal)
Mine, Plasma (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 10d10]] energy damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

 

Mine, plasma

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d10]] energy damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Shield Chassis (Standard).name: Shield Chassis (Standard)
Shield Chassis (Standard).description:
Shield (Any)

Requires attunement

This modifiable shield chassis comes with vacant modification slots for armor modifications. It can house modifications of standard rarity.

Mine, Stun (Deadly).name: Mine, Stun (Deadly)
Mine, Stun (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is stunned until the end of their next turn.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until someone uses an action to shake or slap the creature awake. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Mine, stun

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is stunned until the end of their next turn.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until someone uses an action to shake or slap the creature awake. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Missile, Ion (Deadly).name: Missile, Ion (Deadly)
Missile, Ion (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls made with this enhanced wrist launcher ammunition, which deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 4d6]] ion damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

 

Missile, ion

This wrist launcher ammunition deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Additionally, hit or miss, the missile then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 1d6]] ion damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

Grenade, Fragmentation (Average).name: Grenade, Fragmentation (Average)
Grenade, Fragmentation (Average).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 4d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

 

Grenade, fragmentation

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Deafening Dart (Major).name: Deafening Dart (Major)
Deafening Dart (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this wrist launcher dart, which deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the dart has been fired, it no longer gives a bonus. Also on a hit, the creature must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw, taking [[/r 1d4]] sonic damage on a failed save.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is deafened for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Deafening dart

This wrist launcher ammunition deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Also on a hit, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw, taking [[/r 1d4]] sonic damage on a failed save.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is deafened for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Flechette Mag, Fragmentation (Devastatin.name: Flechette Mag, Fragmentation (Devastating)
Flechette Mag, Fragmentation (Devastatin.description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced flechette cannon ammunition fires a volley of kinetic flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette mag holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 24 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 7d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

 

Flechette mag, fragmentation

When triggered, this flechette cannon ammunition fires a volley of kinetic flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette mag holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Thermal Detonator (Average).name: Thermal Detonator (Average)
Thermal Detonator (Average).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Thermal detonators are unique in that can only be turned off by whoever turned them on. They can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 20 feet must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 4d6]] fire and [[/r 4d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. Additionally, on a failed save, the creature is knocked prone.

 

Thermal detonator

Thermal detonators are unique in that can only be turned off by whoever turned them on. They can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 20 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d6]] fire and [[/r 2d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. Additionally, on a failed save, the creature is knocked prone.

Deafening Calibrator (Major).name: Deafening Calibrator (Major)
Deafening Calibrator (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the sonorous 15 property. If it already has the sonorous property, its sonorous number increases by 3, to a maximum of 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Deafening calibrator

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the sonorous 13 property. If it already has the sonorous property, its sonorous number increases by 1, to a maximum of 18.

Muon Gold (Potent).description:
Consumable (Substance)

Muon gold is a lubricant-based spice that gives users intensified mental clarity and focus for a short time. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next hour, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d10]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 4 hours, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d10]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Muon gold

For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is stunned until the end of their next turn.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until someone uses an action to shake or slap the creature awake. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Mine, Fragmentation (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which activates at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 10d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Grenade Launcher (Superior).name: Grenade Launcher (Superior)
Grenade Launcher (Superior).description:
Weapon (Grenade Launcher)

Rather than traditional power cells, the grenade launcher fires grenades. When firing a grenade at long range, or if you don't meet the grenade launcher's strength requirement, creatures within the radius of the grenade's explosion have advantage on the saving throw. If you lack proficiency in the grenade launcher, you must roll the damage dice twice and take the lesser total. You have a +3 bonus to the save DC and damage rolls of grenades fired by this enhanced grenade launcher.

 

Grenade launcher

Reload 1

A number of shots equal to the reload number can be made with a weapon with this property. A character must then reload it using an action or a bonus action (their choice). You must have one free hand to reload.

Special (range 90/360)

A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has this property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's long range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.

Strength 11

A weapon with this property has a heavy kickback. While wielding it, you have disadvantage on attack rolls unless you meet the Strength requirement. If the weapon has the burst, penetrating, or rapid property, you must meet the Strength requirement to use this feature. If the weapon lacks the two-handed property, you can wield the weapon in two hands to temporarily reduce the strength number by one step (from 19 to 17, 17 to 15, 15 to 13, or 13 to 11). If this would reduce the strength number below 11, you ignore it entirely.

Two-handed

A weapon with this property requires two hands to use.

Description

Rather than traditional power cells, the grenade launcher fires grenades. When firing a grenade at long range, or if you don—t meet the grenade launcher—s strength requirement, creatures within the radius of the grenade—s explosion have advantage on the saving throw. If you lack proficiency in the grenade launcher, you must roll the damage dice twice and take the lesser total.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Tunic Chassis (Standard).name: Tunic Chassis (Standard)
Tunic Chassis (Standard).description:
Adventuring Gear (Body)

Requires attunement

This modifiable clothing chassis comes with vacant modification slots for clothing modifications. It can house modifications of standard rarity.

Grenade, Gas (Major).name: Grenade, Gas (Major)
Grenade, Gas (Major).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. The grenade explodes, filling the air in a 10-foot-radius sphere with yellow-green fog for 1 minute. The fog spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. It lasts for 1 minute or until a wind of at least 10 miles per hour disperses it.

When a creature enters the fog or starts its turn there, it creature must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. The creature takes [[/r 6d6]] poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Additionally, on a failed save, the creature is poisoned while it is in the cloud. Droids, constructs and humanoids wearing appropriate protective equipment are unaffected.

 

Grenade, gas

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. The grenade explodes, filling the air in a 10-foot-radius sphere with yellow-green fog for 1 minute. The fog spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. It lasts for 1 minute or until a wind of at least 10 miles per hour disperses it.

When a creature enters the fog or starts its turn there, it creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. The creature takes [[/r 2d6]] poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Additionally, on a failed save, the creature is poisoned while it is in the cloud. Droids, constructs and humanoids wearing appropriate protective equipment are unaffected.

Matrix Armor.name: Matrix Armor
Matrix Armor.description:
Armor (Any Heavy)

While wearing this suit of armor, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.

This suit of armor has had its protective durasteel components replaced by equivalents made of matrix, a purple metal used to reinforce starships.

Jetpack (Exceptional).name: Jetpack (Exceptional)
Jetpack (Exceptional).description:
Adventuring Gear (Back)

Jetpacks are personal aerial transportation devices that allow the operator to fly into and through the air with great mobility. Activating or deactivating the jetpack requires a bonus action and, while active, you have a flying speed of 70 feet. The jetpack last for 1 minute per power cell (to a maximum of 18 minutes) and can be recharged by a power source or replacing the power cells.

 

Jetpack

Jetpacks are personal aerial transportation devices that allow the operator to fly into and through the air with great mobility. Activating or deactivating the jetpack requires a bonus action and, while active, you have a flying speed of 30 feet. The jetpack last for 1 minute per power cell (to a maximum of 10 minutes) and can be recharged by a power source or replacing the power cells.

Mine, Fragmentation (Devastating).name: Mine, Fragmentation (Devastating)
Mine, Fragmentation (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which activates at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 24 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 12d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

 

Mine, fragmentation

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Electrifying Dart (Major).name: Electrifying Dart (Major)
Electrifying Dart (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this wrist launcher dart, which deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the dart has been fired, it no longer gives a bonus. Also on a hit, the creature must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 1d4]] lightning damage on a failed save.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is shocked for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Electrifying dart

This wrist launcher ammunition deals [[/r 1d6]] kinetic damage on a hit. Also on a hit, the creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 1d4]] lightning damage on a failed save.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is shocked for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Rocketpack (Superior).name: Rocketpack (Superior)
Rocketpack (Superior).description:
Adventuring Gear (Back)

Rocketpacks combine the aerial transportation of a jetpack with the firepower of a rocket launcher. Activating or deactivating the rocketpack's flight capabilities requires a bonus action and, while active, you have a flying speed of 40 feet. The jetpack last for 1 minute per power cell (to a maximum of 8 minutes) and can be recharged by a power source or replacing the power cells.

Additionally, your rocketpack comes with an enhanced integrated rocket launcher with the Ammunition (range 100/400) and reload 1 properties. Rather than traditional power cells, this rocket launcher fires specialized projectiles in the form of rockets. Whenever you would make a ranged weapon attack, you can instead fire this rocket launcher. When firing a rocket at long range, creatures within the radius of the rocket's explosion have advantage on the saving throw. Reloading this rocket launcher takes 1 minute. You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced rocket launcher.

 

Rocketpack

Rocketpacks combine the aerial transportation of a jetpack with the firepower of a rocket launcher. Activating or deactivating the rocketpack's flight capabilities requires a bonus action and, while active, you have a flying speed of 25 feet. The jetpack last for 1 minute per power cell (to a maximum of 5 minutes) and can be recharged by a power source or replacing the power cells.

Additionally, your rocketpack comes with an integrated rocket launcher with the Ammunition (range 100/400) and reload 1 properties. Rather than traditional power cells, this rocket launcher fires specialized projectiles in the form of rockets. Whenever you would make a ranged weapon attack, you can instead fire this rocket launcher. When firing a rocket at long range, creatures within the radius of the rocket's explosion have advantage on the saving throw. Reloading this rocket launcher takes 1 minute.

Projector Tank, Incendiary (Major).name: Projector Tank, Incendiary (Major)
Projector Tank, Incendiary (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced vapor projector ammunition produces a burst of flame in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel tank holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 4d8]] fire damage or half as much on a successful one. The fire spreads around corners. It ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

 

Projector tank, incendiary

When triggered, this vapor projector ammunition produces a burst of flame in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel tank holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 2d8]] fire damage or half as much on a successful one. The fire spreads around corners. It ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

Mine, Gas (Lethal).name: Mine, Gas (Lethal)
Mine, Gas (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, the mine fills the air in a 15-foot-radius sphere with yellow-green fog for 1 minute. The fog spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. It lasts for 1 minute or until a wind of at least 10 miles per hour disperses it.

When a creature enters the fog or starts its turn there, it creature must make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw. The creature takes [[/r 10d6]] poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Additionally, on a failed save, the creature is poisoned while it is in the cloud. Droids, constructs and humanoids wearing appropriate protective equipment are unaffected.

 

Mine, gas

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote

Electrifying Collimator (Average).name: Electrifying Collimator (Average)
Electrifying Collimator (Average).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the shocking 14 property. If it already has the shocking property, its shocking number increases by 2, to a maximum of 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Electrifying collimator

As an action, you can apply this collimator to one lightweapon within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the shocking 13 property. If it already has the shocking property, its shocking number increases by 1, to a maximum of 18.

Wrist-Mounted Grappling Hook.name: Wrist-Mounted Grappling Hook
Wrist-Mounted Grappling Hook.description:
Adventuring Gear (Forearm)

You craft a wrist-mounted grappling hook weapon attached to a tightly coiled cord. With this grappler, you can make a ranged weapon attack with a range of 30/60. On a hit, it deals [[/r 1d4]] kinetic damage. This attack can target a surface, object, or creature.

A creature struck by this attack is impaled by the hook. As an action, a creature can attempt to remove the hook. Removing the hook requires a DC 13 Strength check. While the hook is stuck in the target, you are connected to the target by a 60 foot cable.

While the hook is deployed, you can use your bonus action to activate the reel, pulling yourself to the location if the target is your size or larger. A creature or object smaller than you is pulled to you. Alternatively, you can opt to release the cable (no action required).

Once you've used this feature, you can't use it again until you recover and reinsert the hook as an action.

Reactive Ultrachrome Armor.name: Reactive Ultrachrome Armor
Reactive Ultrachrome Armor.description:
Armor (Heavy Exoskeleton)

Requires attunement

You have resistence to unenhanced damage while you wear this armor. Additionally, you can use an action to make yourself immune to unenhanced damage for 10 minutes or until you are no longer wearing the armor. Once this special action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Made with just enough ultrachrome to give you an edge while still keeping things interesting, these suits of armor can discharge an integrated power cell to catalyze this metal normally used in starship hulls into a more resilient state.

 

Heavy exoskeleton

Bulky

While wearing armor with this property, you have disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks that rely on sound.

Strength 17

While wearing armor or wielding a shield with this property, your speed is reduced by 10 unless your Strength score meets or exceeds the strength number.

Heavy exoskeletons are virtually the heaviest armor acquirable during the Galactic War. It is ideal for extreme combat situations that involved direct damage and also offers a very good level of protection in sacrifice of dexterity. Some consider it claustrophobic but that was the trade-off for safety.

Corrosive Cartridge (Lethal).name: Corrosive Cartridge (Lethal)
Corrosive Cartridge (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced corrosive cartridge. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the corrosive cartridge has been fired, it no longer gives a bonus. Additionally, when you hit with a shot with the loaded weapon, the creature must make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw, taking [[/r 1d10]] acid damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. If you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with the loaded weapon, it must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it dies immediately without making death saving throws. A disintegrated creature and everything unenhanced it is wearing and carrying are reduced to a pile of fine gray dust. A creature destroyed in this way can not be revitalized.

 

Corrosive cartridge

A corrosive cartridge is a specialized slug cartridge for use with blaster weapons that deal kinetic damage. When you hit with a shot with the loaded weapon, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw, taking [[/r 1d8]] acid damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. If you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with the loaded weapon, it must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it dies immediately without making death saving throws. A disintegrated creature and everything unenhanced it is wearing or carrying are reduced to a pile of fine gray dust. A creature destroyed in this way can not be revitalized.

Ring of Apprentice Force Storing.name: Ring of Apprentice Force Storing
Ring of Apprentice Force Storing.description:
Adventuring Gear (Finger)

Requires attunement

This ring stores force powers cast into it, holding them until the attuned wearer uses them. The ring can store up to 3 levels worth of force powers at a time. When found, it contains [[/r 1d4]] - 1 levels of stored force powers chosen by the GM.

Any creature can cast a force power of 1st through 3rd level into the ring by touching the ring as the force power is cast. The force power has no effect, other than to be stored in the ring. If the ring can't hold the force power, the force power is expended without effect. The level of the slot used to cast the force power determines how much space it uses.

While wearing this ring, you can cast any force power stored in it. The force power uses the slot level, force save DC, force attack bonus, and forcecasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the force power. The force power cast from the ring is no longer stored in it, freeing up space.

Slug Cartridge (Deadly).name: Slug Cartridge (Deadly)
Slug Cartridge (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced slug cartridge. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the slug cartridge has been fired, it no longer gives a bonus.

 

Slug cartridge

Weapons with this property fire slug cartridges, which must be loaded in order to fire the weapon.

Cilona (Pure).name: Cilona (Pure)
Cilona (Pure).description:
Consumable (Substance)

Cilona, the active ingredient in deathsticks, is a hallucinogen that causes an ecstatic feeling that improves strength. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next hour, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d12]] when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Strength. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 24 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 8 hours, during which they must roll a [[/r d12]] and subtract the result when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Strength. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 24 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Cilona

Cilona, the active ingredient in deathsticks, is a hallucinogen that causes an ecstatic feeling that improves strength. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d3]] when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Strength. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, during which they must roll a [[/r d3]] and subtract the result when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Strength. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

Mine, Stun (Devastating).name: Mine, Stun (Devastating)
Mine, Stun (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 24 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is stunned until the end of their next turn.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until someone uses an action to shake or slap the creature awake. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Mine, stun

Constitution Stim (Champion).description:
Consumable (Stimpac)

As an action, you can administer this stimpac to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this stimpac, the target's Constitution score increases by 10, and its maximum for this score increases by 10. This effect lasts for 1 hour. A creature can benefit from only one stimpac at a time.

Muon Gold (Pure).name: Muon Gold (Pure)
Muon Gold (Pure).description:
Consumable (Substance)

Muon gold is a lubricant-based spice that gives users intensified mental clarity and focus for a short time. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next hour, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d12]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 24 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 8 hours, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d12]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 24 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Muon gold

Muon gold is a lubricant-based spice that gives users intensified mental clarity and focus for a short time. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

Giggledust (Fine).name: Giggledust (Fine)
Giggledust (Fine).description:
Consumable (Substance)

This sandy-brown powder makes everything humorous while enhancing a creature's nimbleness. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d4]] when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Dexterity. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 1 hour, during which they must roll a [[/r d4]] and subtract the result when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Dexterity. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Giggledust

For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d3]] when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Dexterity. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, during which they must roll a [[/r d3]] and subtract the result when making an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw using Dexterity. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw to resist addiction.

Grenade, Stun (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is stunned until the end of their next turn.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until someone uses an action to shake or slap the creature awake. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Ring of Tech Storing Mk VI.name: Ring of Tech Storing Mk VI
Ring of Tech Storing Mk VI.description:
Adventuring Gear (Finger)

Requires attunement

This ring stores tech powers cast into it, holding them until the attuned wearer uses them. The ring can store up to 9 levels worth of tech powers at a time. When found, it contains [[/r 1d10]] - 1 levels of stored tech powers chosen by the GM.

Any creature can cast a tech power of 1st through 9th level into the ring by touching the ring as the tech power is cast. The tech power has no effect, other than to be stored in the ring. If the ring can't hold the tech power, the tech power is expended without effect. The level of the slot used to cast the tech power determines how much space it uses.

While wearing this ring, you can cast any tech power stored in it. The tech power uses the slot level, tech save DC, tech attack bonus, and techcasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the tech power. The tech power cast from the ring is no longer stored in it, freeing up space.

Strength Enhancer.name: Strength Enhancer
Strength Enhancer.description:
Adventuring Gear (Waist)

Requires attunement

While wearing this belt, your Strength score becomes 21. If your Strength is already equal to or greater than 21, it has no effect on you.

This belt increases a users physical power with pneumaticized assistance joists that provide support and leverage in a minimal form-factor.

Cloak Chassis (Advanced).name: Cloak Chassis (Advanced)
Cloak Chassis (Advanced).description:
Adventuring Gear (Shoulders)

Requires attunement

This modifiable clothing chassis comes with vacant modification slots for clothing modifications, as well as one augment slot. It can house modifications of advanced rarity or lower.

Flechette Clip, Plasma (Devastating).name: Flechette Clip, Plasma (Devastating)
Flechette Clip, Plasma (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced wrist launcher ammunition fires a volley of energy flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette clip holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 24 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 6d10]] energy damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. If you lack proficiency in the wrist launcher, you must roll the damage dice twice and take the lesser total.

 

Flechette clip, plasma

When triggered, this wrist launcher ammunition fires a volley of energy flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette clip holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 1d10]] energy damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. If you lack proficiency in the wrist launcher, you have disadvantage on damage rolls with it

Handwraps Chassis (Premium).description:
Weapon (Unarmed Strike)

Requires attunement

These modifiable handwraps chassis comes with vacant modification slots for vibroweapon modifications. They can house modifications of premium rarity or lower.

Reverse-Engineered Crushgaunts.name: Reverse-Engineered Crushgaunts
Reverse-Engineered Crushgaunts.description:
Adventuring Gear (Hands)

Requires attunement

Your Strength score is 19 while you wear these gauntlets. They have no effect on you if your Strength is already 19 or higher.

Though the knowledge to forge a pair of true shuk'orok is a closely-guarded Mandalorian secret, the effects of these gloves are desirable enough to spawn numerous imitators.

Tihaar

This colorless spirit, favored by Mandalorians, burns the mouth and throat when consumed. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that allows them to roll an additional [[/r d3]] when making an ability check or saving throw using Constitution, and at the start of each of the creature's turns it gains [[/r 2d3]] temporary hit points. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, during which they must roll a [[/r d3]] and subtract the result when making an ability check or saving throw using Constitution, and for the duration their current and maximum hit points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. If this would reduce your maximum hit points below 1, it instead becomes 1. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw to resist addiction.

Grenade, Ion (Deadly).name: Grenade, Ion (Deadly)
Grenade, Ion (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 8d6]] ion damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

 

Grenade, ion

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d6]] ion damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius are disabled until rebooted.

Projector Canister, Cryo (Deadly).name: Projector Canister, Cryo (Deadly)
Projector Canister, Cryo (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced wrist launcher ammunition produces a beam of carbonite energy in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel canister holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes [[/r 4d4]] cold damage and gains 1 slowed level until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't slowed. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, that creature is frozen in carbonite for 1 hour. If you lack proficiency in the wrist launcher, you must roll the damage dice twice and take the lesser total.

 

Projector canister, cryo

When triggered, this wrist launcher ammunition produces a beam of carbonite energy in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel canister holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes [[/r 1d4]] cold damage and gains 1 slowed level until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't slowed. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, that creature is frozen in carbonite for 1 hour. If you lack proficiency in the wrist launcher, you must roll the damage dice twice and take the lesser total.

Deafening Calibrator (Deadly).name: Deafening Calibrator (Deadly)
Deafening Calibrator (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the sonorous 16 property. If it already has the sonorous property, its sonorous number increases by 4, to a maximum of 18. Lastly, if the weapon is not enhanced, it becomes enhanced for the duration.

 

Deafening calibrator

As an action, you can apply this calibrator to one vibroweapon or pair of handwraps within 5 feet, granting it a +1 bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Additionally, for the duration, the weapon gains the sonorous 13 property. If it already has the sonorous property, its sonorous number increases by 1, to a maximum of 18.

Handwraps (Improved).name: Handwraps (Improved)
Handwraps (Improved).description:
Weapon (Unarmed Strike)

While wearing these handwraps, you have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with your unarmed strikes.

Pants Chassis (Legendary).name: Pants Chassis (Legendary)
Pants Chassis (Legendary).description:
Adventuring Gear (Legs)

Requires attunement

This modifiable clothing chassis comes with vacant modification slots for clothing modifications, as well as two augment slots. It can house modifications of legendary rarity or lower.

Novanian Grog (Matured).name: Novanian Grog (Matured)
Novanian Grog (Matured).description:
Consumable (Substance)

This grog features a pungent odor and a strong kick. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next 10 minutes, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d6]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or experience a low that lasts 1 hour, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d6]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Novanian grog

This grog features a pungent odor and a strong kick. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw to resist addiction.

Wisdom Stim (Basic).name: Wisdom Stim (Basic)
Wisdom Stim (Basic).description:
Consumable (Stimpac)

As an action, you can administer this stimpac to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this stimpac, the target's Wisdom score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20. This effect lasts for 1 hour. A creature can benefit from only one stimpac at a time.

Holocron (Adept).name: Holocron (Adept)
Holocron (Adept).description:
Adventuring Gear

This holocron contains a single 5th-level force power, or multiple powers of 1st-4th level with a combined level no greater than 5. After meditating with the holocron for at least 16 hours, a forcecaster with a Max Power Level of 5th or higher can make a DC 22 forcecasting ability check with proficiency based on the alignment of the power(s) stored within. On a success, they learn the contained force power(s), which does not count against their force powers known and can not be unlearned except through use of a remove curse power. On a failure, the power is not learned and the forcecaster can't try again for 24 hours.

Holocrons are information-storage devices used by Force-wielders that contain ancient lessons or valuable information in holographic form.

 

Holocron

Holocrons are information-storage devices used by force wielders that contain ancient lessons or valuable information in holographic form. They appear as palm-sized, glowing polyhedrons of crystalline material and hardware, and can only be activated and used through the power of the Force.

Grenade, Panic (Deadly).name: Grenade, Panic (Deadly)
Grenade, Panic (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 8d6]] psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Grenade, panic

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). As an action, you can prime and throw a grenade at a point you can see within 30 feet + your Strength modifier x 5. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d6]] psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Armor (Exceptional).name: Armor (Exceptional)
Armor (Exceptional).description:
Armor (Any)

You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor. Additionally, while wearing this armor, damage you take is reduced by 1. If this would reduce the damage to 0, the damage is instead reduced to 1.

Net (Superior).name: Net (Superior)
Net (Superior).description:
Weapon (Net)

A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on formless or Huge or larger creatures. A creature can use its action to make a DC 16 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. The net has an AC of 13, 20 hit points, immunity to all damage not dealt by melee weapons, and resistance to damage dealt by unenhanced melee weapons. Destroying the net frees the creature without harming it and immediately ends the net's effects. While a creature is restrained by a net, you can make no further attacks with it.

 

Net

Light

A weapon with this property is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when engaging in Double- or Two-Weapon Fighting. See the rules for Double- and Two-Weapon Fighting in chapter 9.

Finesse

When making an attack with a weapon with this property, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Thrown (range 15)

A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has this property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's long range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.

Special

A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on formless or Huge or larger creatures. A creature can use its action to make a DC 13 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. The net has an AC of 10, 5 hit points, and immunity to all damage not dealt by melee weapons. Destroying the net frees the creature without harming it and immediately ends the net's effects. While a creature is restrained by a net, you can make no further attacks with it.

Flechette Mag, Fragmentation (Deadly).name: Flechette Mag, Fragmentation (Deadly)
Flechette Mag, Fragmentation (Deadly).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced flechette cannon ammunition fires a volley of kinetic flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette mag holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 5d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

 

Flechette mag, fragmentation

When triggered, this flechette cannon ammunition fires a volley of kinetic flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette mag holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d10]] kinetic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Electrifying Cartridge (Lethal).name: Electrifying Cartridge (Lethal)
Electrifying Cartridge (Lethal).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced electrifying cartridge. This bonus is in addition to any bonuses granted by the weapon. Once the electrifying cartridge has been fired, it no longer gives a bonus. Additionally, when you hit with a shot with the loaded weapon, the creature must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d6]] lightning damage and becomes shocked until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't shocked.

 

Electrifying cartridge

An electrifying cartridge is a specialized slug cartridge for use with blaster weapons that deal kinetic damage. When you hit with a shot with the loaded weapon, the creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes [[/r 1d4]] lightning damage and becomes shocked until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't shocked.

Inquisitor Saber.name: Inquisitor Saber
Inquisitor Saber.description:
Weapon (Any Lightweapon)

Requires attunement

Prerequisite: Double property

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enhanced weapon.

As a bonus action, you cause the lightsaber to rapidly spin. When you do so, you lose the +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with the weapon, but you gain a +1 bonus to AC. Additionally, while spinning, you have a 30-foot flying speed. While flying, if you take damage, you must make a concentration check as if concentrating on a power. On a failure, you immediately fall to the ground.

These lightweapons, favored by the Jedi hunters known as Inquisitors, have the unique ability to rapidly spin, granting its wielder a limited flying ability.

Projector Tank, Incendiary (Average).name: Projector Tank, Incendiary (Average)
Projector Tank, Incendiary (Average).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced vapor projector ammunition produces a burst of flame in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel tank holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 3d8]] fire damage or half as much on a successful one. The fire spreads around corners. It ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

 

Projector tank, incendiary

When triggered, this vapor projector ammunition produces a burst of flame in a line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a 15-foot cone. A single fuel tank holds enough fuel for three attacks in a line or a single attack in a cone. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking [[/r 2d8]] fire damage or half as much on a successful one. The fire spreads around corners. It ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

Novanian Grog (Special).name: Novanian Grog (Special)
Novanian Grog (Special).description:
Consumable (Substance)

This grog features a pungent odor and a strong kick. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next hour, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d12]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 24 Constitution saving throw or experience a low that lasts 8 hours, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d12]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 24 Wisdom saving throw to resist addiction.

 

Novanian grog

This grog features a pungent odor and a strong kick. As an action, you can apply this substance to a creature within 5 feet. For the next minute, the creature experiences a high that increases their current and maximum tech points by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the high, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or experience a low that lasts 10 minutes, their current and maximum tech points are reduced by [[/r 2d3]]. At the end of the low, the creature must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw to resist addiction.

Mine, Deafening (Major).name: Mine, Deafening (Major)
Mine, Deafening (Major).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 6d6]] sonic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is deafened for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

 

Mine, deafening

Mines can be set to detonate when a creature comes within up to 15 feet of it or paired with a remote detonator. As an action, you can prime and set a mine on a surface you can reach, which arms at the start of your next turn. When detonated, each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d6]] sonic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature is deafened for 1 minute. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Flechette Mag, Plasma (Major).description:
Consumable (Ammunition)

When triggered, this enhanced flechette cannon ammunition fires a volley of energy flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette mag holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 4d10]] energy damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

 

Flechette mag, plasma

When triggered, this flechette cannon ammunition fires a volley of energy flechette rounds in a short line 15 feet long and 5 feet wide or a long line 45 feet long and 5 feet wide. A single flechette mag holds enough ammunition for three attacks in a short line or one attack in a long line. Each creature must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes [[/r 2d10]] energy damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Flechette Mag, Ion (Major).description:

A creature takes [[/r 4d6]] ion damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. Flechette mag, ion

A creature takes [[/r 2d6]] ion damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one. Any electronics within the blast radius that aren't being worn or carried are disabled until rebooted.

Dexterity Stim (Champion).name: Dexterity Stim (Champion)
Dexterity Stim (Champion).description:
Consumable (Stimpac)

As an action, you can administer this stimpac to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this stimpac, the target's Dexterity score increases by 10, and its maximum for this score increases by 10. This effect lasts for 1 hour. A creature can benefit from only one stimpac at a time.

Remote Detonator (Improved).name: Remote Detonator (Improved)
Remote Detonator (Improved).description:
Adventuring Gear

A small handhold device with a single button, used to activate explosives. Over the course of 1 minute, you can pair the detonator with up to six mines within 10 feet. As an action, you can remotely detonate all of the paired mines. You must be within 90 feet of the mines to detonate them.

 

Remote detonator

A small handhold device with a single button, used to activate explosives. Over the course of 1 minute, you can pair the detonator with up to two mines within 10 feet. As an action, you can remotely detonate all of the paired mines. You must be within 30 feet of the mines to detonate them.

Poison (Average).name: Poison (Average)
Poison (Average).description:
Consumable (Poison)

As an action, you can use the poison in this vial to coat one vibroweapon, one slug cartridge, or one wrist launcher dart. A creature hit by the poisoned weapon must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw, taking [[/r 4d4]] poison damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Once applied, the poison retains potency for 1 minute before drying.

 

Poison

As an action, you can use the poison in this vial to coat one vibroweapon, one slug cartridge, or one wrist launcher dart. A creature hit by the poisoned weapon must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw, taking [[/r 2d4]] poison damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. Once applied, the poison retains potency for 1 minute before drying.

Alacrity Adrenal (Superior).name: Alacrity Adrenal (Superior)
Alacrity Adrenal (Superior).description:
Consumable (Adrenal)

As an action, you can administer this adrenal to a creature within 5 feet. When you administer this adrenal, the target's speed increases by 40 feet. This effect lasts for 1 minute. A creature can benefit from only one adrenal at a time.

Portable Teleporter Mk II.name: Portable Teleporter Mk II
Portable Teleporter Mk II.description:
Consumable (Technology)

As an action, you can use this teleporter to create a pair of linked portals: one portal appears in a space within 5 feet of you, and the other portal appears in an unoccupied space you can see up to 60 feet away. These portals lasts until the start of your next turn, and they are large enough to accommodate Medium and smaller creatures and objects. Portals take the appearance of an elongated, shimmering mirror, and looking through a portal, a creature can see through the linked portal as if looking through a window. A creature or object who passes through a portal immediately appears in a space within 5 feet of the linked portal.

Grenade, Panic (Devastating).name: Grenade, Panic (Devastating)
Grenade, Panic (Devastating).description:
Consumable (Explosive)

Grenades can be set to detonate on impact or with a timer that causes them to explode on initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties). A creature takes [[/r 12d6]] psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much as on a successful one.

Additionally, on a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. At the start of an affected creature's turn, it can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.
